contracts.note: The 6-day PLTR $165 calls traded heavy on the bid this hour, with volume at 4.5x prior open interest, suggesting sellers are exiting positions rather than new buyers entering. The 5.5% out-of-the-money strike and 56% implied volatility, coupled with a $1.9M premium, indicate a tactical bet on a significant move within the week, though the bid tape suggests it's not an aggressive new long position.

NVDA call buyers are lifting offers in size, with today's volume at 3.4x open interest and an additional 4.2k contracts printed this hour. The 6.6% out-of-the-money strike with only 4 days to expiration and a $2.2M premium suggests a tactical bet on an immediate upward move, with the 54% implied volatility indicating the market is already pricing in a significant price swing.

NVDA call buyers are lifting offers in size — 25k contracts traded today vs only 4k of open interest going in, with an extra 7k contracts coming in this hour alone. A $0.9M premium check on a 5% OTM strike with one day to expiration is a tactical bet on an outsized intraday move, not patient positioning, and the 43% IV suggests the market is pricing in a move.

NVDA call buyers are aggressively lifting offers on a 1-day to expiration strike, with today's volume already more than double the open interest and an additional 6k contracts printed this hour. The 6.6% out-of-the-money strike and 46% implied volatility suggest a tactical bet on a significant intraday move, funded by a modest $0.5M premium.

FTNT call buyers are aggressively lifting offers 5% out of the money with only 5 days to expiration, and this hour's volume is 300x prior open interest, indicating new positions are being opened. The $0.7M premium suggests a conviction on an outsized move, and the 51% IV implies the market is already pricing in significant volatility.

QQQ put buyers are hitting bids aggressively, with today's volume already 2x open interest and an additional 5,000 contracts printed this hour. This 22% out-of-the-money strike with only one day to expiration, despite the high 108% IV, represents a very tactical, high-conviction bet on an immediate and significant downside move.

Buyers are aggressively lifting offers on the 5-day CRM $205 call, with today's volume already 17x open interest and an additional 4k contracts traded this hour. The 7% out-of-the-money strike and 52% implied volatility suggest a tactical bet on a significant move higher into expiration, funded by a $1.4M premium check.
